summary: The diff implements session persistence fixes across App.tsx, LoginPage.tsx, DashboardPage.tsx, AdminPanel.tsx, and useQueries.ts. REQ-1 (loading spinner blocking render during isInitializing), REQ-2 (queries gated on !isInitializing to prevent anonymous actor cache entries, AdminPanel checking isInitializing before access-denied), and REQ-3 (LoginPage showing loading state and redirecting via useEffect when already authenticated) are all addressed. However, the diff does not show any changes to useActor.ts or a wrapper/alternative that ensures the actor is initialized with the restored authenticated identity — a key REQ-2 acceptance criterion. The constraints prohibit modifying useActor.ts directly but require a safe alternative or wrapper fix in other files, and no such wrapper is present in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: The backend actor is initialized with the restored authenticated identity so that canister calls carry the correct principal. The useActor hook correctly picks up the persisted identity on mount and does not fall back to anonymous principal for authenticated sessions.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff gates queries on !isInitializing and !actorFetching, but no wrapper or alternative to useActor.ts is introduced to ensure the actor itself is constructed with the restored identity rather than an anonymous one. Without fixing actor initialization, canister calls may still carry the anonymous principal even after identity restoration completes.
